findings.summary: §1 resolved the CLI path from process.argv[1] with a vfkb.mjs-sibling preference, so the npm/dist MCP face emitted `node .../dist/mcp-server.js add …` — a command that starts a stdio server, blocks on stdin and writes nothing; the pi/embedded face named the host's script. Now anchored to the engine module's own location with a CLI_FACES candidate list; verified live on 4 faces incl. an npm -g symlink prefix

the path guard was vacuous — 'non-empty' and 'starts with /' were both true of the broken mcp-server.js path; the guarantee now asserted at built-artifact altitude via kb_resume plus a resolveCliFace layout matrix, and breaking the injectable default alone goes red

§2a floor was kb_add-shaped though RFC-035 says 'every engine throw'; swallowing kb_supersede/kb_transition left the suite green. Table-driven over the write tools. NOTE: the first cut of this test itself passed for the wrong reason (passed `id` where kb_supersede takes `old_id` → schema rejection, never reached the engine); fixed and content-asserted against the engine's own complaint
absolute-ising of VFKB_DATA_DIR is off-spec (justified) but shipped with a test that could not detect its removal since every arm used an absolute brain dir; added a relative-'.vfkb' arm
the write-impossible arm only exercised file creation (dir chmod, no log); added the #176 field shape — an existing entries.jsonl made read-only
suite drives dist/, so running it alone against a stale build reports a false green; documented at the serverPath line (CI builds first, so CI is honest)

an auto-generated PR-body block claimed a behaviour change ('MCP add failures now report explicit errors instead of appearing successful') that the diff does not make; removed and explicitly corrected in the body

cosmetic: the unresolved-CLI marker renders as `node <vfkb CLI not found…>`, which bash parses as an input redirection — it fails loudly (the correct outcome) but incidentally rather than by design; dropping the leading `node ` would make it unpasteable on purpose
